hero.headline: See the patterns your mind keeps repeating.
hero.subheadline: Take the 60-second quiz, get a sample pattern profile, and see the AI dashboard we are building for thoughts, habits, goals, and decisions.
hero.social_proof: Early-access waitlist only. No live app access yet.

f1.title: Planned input modes
f1.body: MindMirror is being designed around voice first, with room to support text, photos, and video over time.

f2.title: Searchable memory
f2.body: The product direction is to turn scattered reflections into something you can search, revisit, and understand over time.

f3.title: Pattern detection
f3.body: The core promise being tested: show which topics, problems, goals, and decisions keep coming back.

f4.title: Honest pattern insight
f4.body: Less vague positivity. More: 'This keeps appearing when you talk about work decisions.'

f5.title: Life dashboard direction
f5.body: The long-term idea is to connect habits, milestones, goals, and recurring life areas in one place.

f6.title: Your data, forever
f6.body: Export everything anytime. Your thoughts belong to you.

social_proof.quote1: Most journaling tools help you record thoughts. MindMirror is built to show what keeps repeating.
social_proof.quote2: The quiz turns vague overthinking into a named pattern you can actually inspect.
social_proof.quote3: The page is intentionally clear: this is early-access validation, not a live app launch.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: Choose a plan to join the early-access waitlist. MindMirror is not a live app yet.
pricing.card_status: Early-access waitlist reservation. No live app access yet.
pricing.trial_days: {days}-day Stripe trial before billing; waitlist only today
pricing.empty_state: No plans synced yet. In Stripe, tag products with metadata foundation = true, add price lookup_key and metadata internal_id, then run npm run stripe:sync.

features.alerts: Planned pattern alerts when a topic repeats

faq.q1: Is this just another journaling app?
faq.a1: No. Most journaling apps store your thoughts. MindMirror is being built to analyze them over time and surface patterns. It's the difference between a diary and a dashboard.
faq.q2: How does the pattern detection work?
faq.a2: The intended product analyzes entries for topics, goals, emotions, and recurring phrases. Over time, it should connect the dots and show what keeps coming up.
faq.q3: Do I have to write or type anything?
faq.a3: The planned product is voice-first, so the core experience should be pressing record and speaking instead of forcing a writing habit.
faq.a4: For the early-access demand test, checkout is waitlist-only and refundable where stated. Full subscription management will be handled when the live product ships.
faq.a5: Your entries are private and encrypted. We never sell or share your data. You can export and delete everything at any time.

how_it_works.title: How it works
how_it_works.subtitle: The quiz shows a preview now. The full product is being designed around three steps.
how_it_works.step1_body: In the full product, you would record a thought in 30 seconds. Say what's on your mind, what happened, or what you're worried about.
how_it_works.step2_body: Entries would be transcribed and analyzed for topics, emotions, and goals, then compared for what keeps coming up.
how_it_works.step3_body: MindMirror is being built to show patterns you may not notice: goals you mention but don't act on, problems you revisit, and themes that hold you back.

meta.title: MindMirror
meta.description: A voice-first AI journaling concept designed to detect thought patterns over time and show what keeps coming back.
meta.home_title: See the patterns your mind keeps repeating
meta.home_description: Take the MindMirror quiz to preview a thought-pattern result and join the early-access waitlist.
meta.pricing_description: Early-access plans for the MindMirror waitlist. The live app is still being built.
meta.privacy_description: How MindMirror handles your data and protects your privacy.
